researched_category.value: Communications Platform as a Service (CPaaS) – Programmable Voice, SMS, Video, Verify (2FA), and multi-channel messaging APIs
researched_category.notes: Vonage is a CPaaS provider offering APIs for voice, SMS, video, verify, email, and messaging across multiple channels.

one_liner.value: Cloud communications platform providing programmable APIs for voice, SMS, video, two-factor authentication, and omnichannel messaging.

methods.notes: Vonage supports multiple auth methods: API Key/Secret via Basic Auth (Base64), JWT Bearer tokens (recommended for production), and CAMARA OAuth2 Client Credentials flow. Some APIs also support HMAC/Signature authentication.

access.notes: Anyone can sign up for a free Vonage API account at dashboard.nexmo.com. The account comes with €2 free test credit. Phone verification is required (PIN sent via SMS or call). No credit card is needed for the trial. The account starts in DEMO mode (limited to 10 destination numbers). To move to production, you add credit (pay-as-you-go). There is no admin or sales gate for basic API access.

summary.value: Vonage has a comprehensive suite of REST APIs documented with OpenAPI specifications. APIs cover: SMS, MMS, Voice (programmable/SIP), Video, Verify (2FA), Messages (multi-channel: WhatsApp, RCS, Facebook Messenger, Viber, Email), Number Insight, Number Management, Account management, Reports, Audit, Media, Conversation, Dispatch, and more. Each API has full OpenAPI specs, Postman collections, official SDKs in 6+ languages, and extensive documentation at developer.vonage.com.

agent_interface.notes: Vonage provides official MCP servers: (1) Documentation MCP Server (GitHub: Vonage/vonage-mcp-server-documentation) – official, by Vonage; (2) Server API Bindings MCP Server – official; (3) Telephony MCP Server (GitHub: Vonage-Community/telephony-mcp-server) – community but from Vonage-Community org. Vonage also has official SDKs for Node.js, Python, PHP, Ruby, Java, .NET, Go, and a CLI tool. The developer site has a dedicated MCP Server section.

rationale.value: Vonage is fully buildable as an agent toolkit today. It has a well-documented public REST API with OpenAPI specs, multiple authentication options (API key/secret for Basic Auth, JWT for production), a self-serve free tier with €2 trial credit and no credit card required (phone verification only), and official MCP servers already published by Vonage. The API surface is broad (SMS, Voice, Video, Verify, Messages, Numbers, etc.) and all endpoints are accessible via standard REST calls. An agent toolkit can use the existing MCP servers or build on top of the REST API directly using JWT or API Key authentication.

s1.quote: Vonage APIs support different authentication methods depending on which product you are using: API Key and Secret (Header) | JSON Web Token (JWT). Messages supports both JWT and Basic authentication... For most use-cases we recommend JWT Authentication.
s1.title: Authentication - API Concepts | Vonage API Documentation

s2.quote: Vonage provides a suite of APIs that enable you to communicate with your customers using your preferred channels. Signing up for an account... will give you an API key and secret... We'll give you some free credit when you first open your account.
s2.title: Getting Started With Vonage APIs (New User Guide)

s3.quote: The CAMARA APIs can additionally be accessed using the OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow by providing a JWT Bearer token to the OAuth Token endpoint.
s3.title: CAMARA OAuth2 Scheme | Vonage API Documentation

s4.quote: The Vonage MCP Server enables AI agents and developer tools to discover, describe, and use Vonage capabilities via the Model Context Protocol. Agent-native docs search (Documentation MCP). Tool discovery & granular permissions. IDE & Claude Desktop integration.
s4.title: Vonage MCP Server Overview | Vonage MCP Server Documentation

s5.quote: The Vonage MCP Server allows AI agents and developer tools to interact with Vonage APIs through the Model Context Protocol... By implementing MCP, Vonage makes its communication capabilities (Voice, Messaging, Video, and more) discoverable and usable in agentic environments.
s5.title: Technical Details | Vonage MCP Server

s6.quote: To create your free Vonage account: ... Vonage sends a PIN to your phone as a text message or automated phone call. ... When you create your Vonage account you are given €2 free test credit and your account is set in DEMO mode.
s6.title: Using the Vonage Dashboard for Account Management

s7.quote: Access a host of developer resources, with free evaluation trials
s7.title: Communications APIs | Vonage

s8.quote: For products that use Basic Authentication, the Ruby SDK sets an Authorization header... Vonage APIs may require your API Key and Secret... Basic Authentication, JWT Authentication, Signature Authentication.
s8.title: README – Documentation for vonage/vonage-ruby-sdk

s9.quote: Try it free. Start developing now with free credit. No credit card required.
s9.title: Voice API Pricing | Vonage

s10.quote: Vonage Communications APIs... Messaging (RCS, SMS/MMS, WhatsApp, Email)... Voice API... Video API... Verify API... Fraud Protection... AI Studio... Reports
s10.title: Vonage Communications APIs

s11.title: How much does the Vonage SMS API cost? (2026 pricing)

s12.quote: An MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that provides AI assistants with access to Vonage API documentation, code snippets, tutorials, and troubleshooting resources.
s12.title: Vonage Documentation MCP Server

s13.quote: This directory contains MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server tools for telephony operations, such as making voice calls and sending SMS messages using the Vonage API.
s13.title: Telephony MCP Server

limitations: I could not independently verify the exact pricing structure for all APIs (some pages use dynamic rendering), but the overall pay-as-you-go model is confirmed.
The Vonage MCP Server 'Server API Bindings' details were not deeply explored, but the existence of official MCP servers is confirmed.
The exact limits of the free trial (€2, 10 destination numbers) are confirmed from official docs.
